perturbation.original_text: Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, the Joint Venturers shall not be required to make any capital contribution to the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, each Joint Venturer shall be required to make an initial capital contribution of $10,000 to the Joint Venture.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 3 initially states that no capital contribution is required unless mutually agreed upon. The modified text, however, mandates an initial capital contribution of $10,000 from each Joint Venturer. This contradicts the original clause and creates uncertainty about the capital contribution obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as agreed upon by mutual consent, each Joint Venturer shall be required to make an initial capital contribution of $10,000 to the Joint Venture. 4. Offices of the Joint Venture. The principal place of business of the Joint Venture shall be at 1600 Lower State Road, in the City of Doylestown, Bucks County, Pennsylvania, but may maintain such other offices as the Joint Venturers may deem advisable at any other place or places within or without the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. 5. Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers. The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall, except as provided for below, have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made. 39
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that no capital contribution is required unless mutually agreed upon. The changed text mandates an initial capital contribution of $10,000 from each Joint Venturer. This directly contradicts the original clause, creating an in-text contradiction as it removes the 'unless mutually agreed upon' condition, forcing a contribution.

perturbation.original_text: Each distribution shall be made ratably to the Joint Venturers according to their prorata interest in the Joint Venture as shown in Section 6.
perturbation.changed_text: Each distribution shall be made at the discretion of CCGI, prioritizing its operational needs, with any remaining funds distributed to the Joint Venturers according to their prorata interest in the Joint Venture as shown in Section 6.
perturbation.explanation: The inserted text creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, Section 9 mandates that distributions be made ratably based on prorata interest. The modified text grants CCGI the discretion to prioritize distributions based on its operational needs before prorata distribution. This contradicts the initial provision and creates an imbalance of power, potentially leading to disputes over the fairness and timing of distributions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributions may only be made if after any distribution is made, the Joint Venture assets are in excess of all liabilities of the Joint Venture. Each distribution shall be made at the discretion of CCGI, prioritizing its operational needs, with any remaining funds distributed to the Joint Venturers according to their prorata interest in the Joint Venture as shown in Section 6. 10. Internal Revenue Code Election. The Joint Venturers agree and declare that this association for the carrying on of a joint venture business operation does not, and is not intended to create a partnership, for either legal or United States income tax purposes, each Party recognizing that the other is willing and able to contribute capital, labor, and services for the operation of a successful joint venture business. Further, each Party elects under the authority of Section 761(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (the "Code"), as amended and all successor statutes, to be excluded from the application of all of the provisions of Subchapter K of Chapter 1 of the Subtitle A of the Code, and the Parties agree that the election out of Subchapter K of Chapter 1 of Subtitle A of the Code shall, if necessary, be manifested by their execution and filing of all appropriate documentation. The Parties also declare that they are not making any agreement to undertake any business other than that set forth in this Agreement; and nothing in this Agreement is to be construed as a limitation of the powers or rights of either Party to carry on his separate business for his sole benefit; provided, however, the Parties shall cooperate with each other according to the terms and spirit of this Agreement in the performance of their joint venture business operation. 11. Procedure on Termination and Liquidation. On any termination of the Joint Venture, its debt shall be paid or provided for in a manner satisfactory to the Joint Venturers. Then, any unexpended portion of Joint Venture funds shall be distributed to the Joint Venturers in accordance with their prorata ownership in the Joint Venture and all other assets of the Joint Venture shall be distributed as undivided interests to the Joint Venturers ratably according to their prorata interests in the Joint Venture as set forth in Section 6. If any asset is not capable of being distributed on an undivided basis, the Parties shall agree on a price for such asset and it shall be distributed to one Party and a corresponding balance, in cash or property, shall be made of the Joint Venture assets so that each Party receives his proportionate share of all the Joint Venture assets. 12. Sale or Purchase of Interest of Joint Venturer Prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants CCGI the discretion to prioritize distributions based on its operational needs before prorata distribution. Section 11 of the 'Contradicted Text' states, 'Then, any unexpended portion of Joint Venture funds shall be distributed to the Joint Venturers in accordance with their prorata ownership in the Joint Venture.' The modified text in Section 9 directly contradicts this, as it allows CCGI to prioritize its needs, potentially diminishing or delaying the prorata distribution mandated in Section 11. This creates a conflict over the distribution process, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
